# Architecture Export Skill

> **Last updated:** 2026-04-06

Scans the codebase, generates an updated architecture document, and produces a polished, professionally styled PowerPoint presentation — all in one automated workflow.

---

## Overview

This skill automates two tightly coupled tasks:

1. **Architecture Document Refresh** — Scan every system file, detect what changed, and regenerate the canonical architecture markdown document (`Docs/Application-Architecture.md`).
2. **PDF + PowerPoint Generation** — Convert that document into a PDF and a ~22-slide PPTX using the generator scripts bundled in this skill folder.

Both phases run sequentially and are always executed together. Whenever the architecture document is created or refreshed (Phase 1), the PDF and PPTX exports are automatically regenerated (Phase 2) so all three files stay in sync.

---

## Prerequisites

> **Note:** The PDF and PPTX are written directly to `Docs/` (the same folder as the architecture document). The generator scripts live permanently in this skill folder and are never deleted.

| Requirement | Details |
|-------------|-------|
| Node.js | v18+ |
| pptxgenjs | v4+ (`npm install` from repo root) |
| pdfkit | v0.14+ (`npm install` from repo root) |
| Architecture doc | `Docs/Application-Architecture.md` (generated by Phase 1) |
| PPTX generator | `.github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-deck.js` |
| PDF generator | `.github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-pdf.js` |
| Section catalog | `references/ARCHITECTURE_SECTIONS.md` |

---

## Canonical Section List (15 Sections)

The architecture document always contains exactly these 15 sections in this order:

| # | Section Title | Primary Source Files |
|---|--------------|---------------------|
| 1 | Executive Summary | `README.md`, repo root |
| 2 | Repository Structure | Filesystem scan — `src/`, `infra/`, `.github/`, `playwright/` |
| 3 | Application Architecture | `src/web/Website/Program.cs`, `src/web/Data/Repositories/` |
| 4 | Source Code Projects | All `.csproj` files across `src/` |
| 5 | AI/GenAI Integration | `src/web/Website/Repositories/AIHelper.cs`, `src/web/Website/*.Web.csproj` |
| 6 | Infrastructure as Code (Bicep) | `infra/Bicep/main.bicep`, `infra/Bicep/modules/`, `infra/Bicep/resourcenames.bicep` |
| 7 | CI/CD Pipelines | `.github/workflows/`, `.azuredevops/pipelines/` |
| 8 | Testing | `src/web/Tests/`, `src/function/Tests/`, `playwright/`, `playwright.config.*.ts` |
| 9 | Authentication & Security | `src/web/Website/Helpers/AuthorizeApiKey.cs`, `src/web/Website/Program.cs` |
| 10 | Theme and UI | `src/web/Website/Pages/`, `src/web/Website/Components/`, `wwwroot/` |
| 11 | Configuration Reference | `src/web/Website/applicationSettings.json`, `infra/Bicep/main.bicep` params |
| 12 | Key Design Patterns | `src/web/Data/Repositories/`, `src/web/Website/Pages/`, `src/web/Website/Program.cs` |
| 13 | Copilot / GitHub Copilot Configuration | `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.github/skills/`, `.github/instructions/`, `.github/agents/` |
| 14 | Azure Developer CLI (AZD) Integration | `azure.yaml`, `infra/azd-main.bicep`, `.github/workflows/azure-dev.yml` |
| 15 | Documentation | `Docs/` (all `.md` files) |

---

## Workflow — 10 Steps (2 Phases)

### Phase 1: Architecture Document Refresh

#### Step 1 — Scan the Codebase

Read all source files that feed into the architecture document:

| Category | Files / Paths |
|----------|--------------|
| Web app | `src/web/Website/Program.cs`, `src/web/Website/*.csproj`, `src/web/Website/Pages/`, `src/web/Website/Components/`, `src/web/Website/API/`, `src/web/Website/Repositories/AIHelper.cs` |
| Data library | `src/web/Data/*.csproj`, `src/web/Data/Models/`, `src/web/Data/Repositories/IJokeRepository.cs` |
| Function | `src/function/Function/*.csproj`, `src/function/Function/TriggerHttp.cs`, `src/function/Function/TriggerHealthCheck.cs` |
| MCP servers | `src/mcp/*.cs`, `src/mcp/*MCPSSE/` |
| Console + Analyzer | `src/console/`, `src/analyzer/` |
| SQL project | `src/sql.database/sql.database.sqlproj`, `src/sql.database/dbo/` |
| Infrastructure | `infra/Bicep/main.bicep`, `infra/Bicep/modules/`, `infra/Bicep/resourcenames.bicep`, `infra/Bicep/data/` |
| CI/CD | `.github/workflows/` (all `.yml` files), `.azuredevops/pipelines/` |
| Testing | `src/web/Tests/`, `src/function/Tests/`, `playwright/`, all `playwright.config.*.ts` |
| Security/Auth | `src/web/Website/Helpers/AuthorizeApiKey.cs`, `src/web/Website/Helpers/Utilities.cs` |
| Copilot config | `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.github/skills/`, `.github/instructions/`, `.github/agents/` |
| AZD | `azure.yaml`, `infra/azd-main.bicep`, `infra/azd-main.parameters.json` |
| Documentation | `Docs/` (all `.md` files), `README.md` |

#### Step 2 — Read Existing Architecture Doc

Load `Docs/Application-Architecture.md` as the baseline document. Parse all 15 sections (see the Canonical Section List above and `references/ARCHITECTURE_SECTIONS.md` for per-section source details).

Preserve the document's existing structure, tone, and formatting conventions:
- `## N. Section Title` heading format
- ASCII box diagrams where present
- Tables for structured data (packages, modules, workflows, pages, etc.)
- Version / generation metadata block at the top

#### Step 3 — Detect Changes

For each of the 15 canonical sections, compare the current codebase state against what the architecture doc currently describes.

Flag sections as:
- **🟢 Current** — No changes detected
- **🟡 Stale** — Minor updates needed (new values, version bumps, counts changed)
- **🔴 Outdated** — Structural changes, new features, or removed components

#### Step 4 — Refresh Architecture Doc

Update all 15 sections with current data from the codebase. For each section:

1. Re-read the source files listed in the Canonical Section List and `references/ARCHITECTURE_SECTIONS.md`
2. Regenerate content from source-of-truth data (don't just patch — rebuild from data)
3. Preserve ASCII diagrams and update them if the underlying structure changed
4. Update all counts, lists, package versions, and tables to reflect current state
5. Add new subsections if the codebase has new features not yet documented

**Important:** Never remove sections — the 15-section structure is canonical. If a section has no changes, keep it as-is.

#### Step 5 — Save Architecture Doc

Write the refreshed document to `Docs/Application-Architecture.md`.

- Update the version metadata line at the top: `> **Version:** X.Y · Generated: YYYY-MM-DD`
- Increment the patch version if only minor/stale updates were made; increment the minor version for structural changes
- Verify the file was written successfully and report byte count
- **After saving, automatically proceed to Phase 2** — export generation always follows a document refresh

---

### Phase 2: PDF + PowerPoint Generation

> **This phase runs automatically after every Phase 1 document refresh.** No manual triggering is needed.

#### Step 6 — Check for Generator Scripts

**Before generating any output, check whether the generator scripts already exist:**

```bash
# Check for bundled generator scripts
test -f .github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-deck.js
test -f .github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-pdf.js
```

- **If both scripts exist** → skip to Step 8 (run them directly — do NOT regenerate or overwrite)
- **If a script is missing** → report which script is missing and stop; the scripts are bundled with this skill and should not be recreated automatically

#### Step 7 — Read Template Specs (if reviewing styles)

Apply the following brand guidelines for the presentation:

- 7 slide master layouts (Title, Section Divider, Content, Two-Column, Diagram, Table, Closing)
- Segoe UI font family (Semibold for headers, Regular for body)
- Brand color palette (Primary: #0078D4)
- Accessibility requirements (contrast ratios ≥ 4.5:1, minimum 18pt font, alt text on diagrams)
- Slide dimensions: 16:9 widescreen (13.33" × 7.5")

#### Step 8 — Map Sections to Slides (reference only — handled by script)

The generator scripts handle all layout mapping automatically. For reference, the section-to-slide mapping is:

| Section | Slide Layout | Est. Slides |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| 1. Executive Summary | Title + Content | 2 |
| 2. Repository Structure | Diagram | 1 |
| 3. Application Architecture | Diagram + Content | 2 |
| 4. Source Code Projects | Two-Column (×3 subsections) | 3 |
| 5. AI/GenAI Integration | Diagram + Table | 2 |
| 6. Infrastructure as Code | Diagram + Table | 2 |
| 7. CI/CD Pipelines | Table (×2 platforms) | 2 |
| 8. Testing | Two-Column | 1 |
| 9. Authentication & Security | Diagram + Content | 1 |
| 10. Theme and UI | Content | 1 |
| 11. Configuration Reference | Table | 1 |
| 12. Key Design Patterns | Table | 1 |
| 13. Copilot Configuration | Content | 1 |
| 14. AZD Integration | Table | 1 |
| 15. Documentation | Table | 1 |

Target: ~22 slides total (varies with content). The generator script handles detailed layout selection.

#### Step 9 — Run the Generator Scripts

Determine the `--title` (the application display name, e.g. from the README or repo name) and `--repo` (the `owner/repo-name` identifier) from the repository context, then run both scripts from the repo root:

```bash
# Generate PPTX
node .github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-deck.js \
  --input  "Docs/Application-Architecture.md" \
  --output "Docs/Application-Architecture.pptx" \
  --title  "<App Display Name>" \
  --repo   "<owner/repo-name>"

# Generate PDF
node .github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-pdf.js \
  --input  "Docs/Application-Architecture.md" \
  --output "Docs/Application-Architecture.pdf" \
  --title  "<App Display Name>" \
  --repo   "<owner/repo-name>"
```

The scripts:
- Parse the markdown document into 15 sections
- Apply template spec formatting (colors, typography, layouts)
- Output `Docs/Application-Architecture.pptx` (~22 slides, ~750 KB)
- Output `Docs/Application-Architecture.pdf` (~12 pages, ~45 KB)

#### Step 10 — Verify Output

Validate both generated files:

1. **Files exist** — Check `Docs/Application-Architecture.pptx` and `Docs/Application-Architecture.pdf`
2. **File sizes** — PPTX should be > 100 KB; PDF should be > 10 KB
3. **No errors** — Both generator exit codes are 0

If verification fails, capture stderr and report the error.

#### Step 11 — Report Completion

Present a completion summary:

```
📊 Architecture Export Complete

Document: Docs/Application-Architecture.md
  - Sections refreshed: X/15
  - Sections unchanged: Y/15
  - New content added: [list if any]

Deck:  Docs/Application-Architecture.pptx  (~22 slides, XXX KB)
PDF:   Docs/Application-Architecture.pdf   (~12 pages, XX KB)

Generator scripts used (pre-existing — not regenerated):
  .github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-deck.js
  .github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/generate-architecture-pdf.js

Changes detected:
  🟡 Section 4 (Source Code Projects) — package version bump
  🔴 Section 7 (CI/CD Pipelines) — new workflow added
  🟢 All other sections current
```

---

## Integration

This skill can be invoked directly or as part of a release documentation workflow.

**Trigger phrases:**
- "refresh architecture deck"
- "generate architecture export"
- "update architecture pptx"
- "architecture refresh"
- "rebuild architecture doc"
- "export architecture"
- "generate deck"
- "update the architecture doc"
- "create architecture presentation"

---

## Error Handling

| Error | Recovery |
|-------|----------|
| Missing source files | Report which files are missing; generate doc with available data and flag gaps |
| Node.js not available | Complete Phase 1 only; skip export generation with a note |
| pptxgenjs not installed | Run `npm install pptxgenjs` from repo root |
| pdfkit not installed | Run `npm install pdfkit` from repo root |
| Generator script missing | Report missing script path — do NOT recreate; scripts live in `.github/skills/architecture-export/scripts/` |
| Generator script fails | Capture stderr, report error, suggest running manually from repo root |

---
